title: Smoky Silky Chinese Cashews Stir-fry
description: A nutty, silky stirfry that blends roasted cashews, sweet muscovado, and a whisper of liquid smoke for an adventurous Chineseinspired snack.
instructions: Rinse the cashews under cold water and pat dry with a towel.
Heat 1 tablespoon of sesame oil in a dry wok over medium‑high heat. Add the cashews and stir‑fry for 3–4 minutes until they turn golden brown and fragrant.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the wok, cooking for 30 seconds until aromatic.
Pour in the soy sauce, muscovado sugar, and liquid smoke, stirring to coat the cashews and allow the sauce to thicken slightly, about 1 minute.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the remaining tablespoon of sesame oil, mixing thoroughly to create a silky glaze.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, ensuring the cashews remain coated and the sauce is glossy.
Transfer the stir‑fry to a serving bowl, then sprinkle the sliced green onions and a pinch of black pepper on top.
Serve warm as a savory snack or side dish.
